claim: codex-plugin-cc is an official plugin built and published by OpenAI that allows developers to invoke Codex from inside Claude Code CLI.
The plugin was released on March 30-31, 2026 under the Apache 2.0 license. Version v1.0.2 patched the same day.
The plugin exposes six slash commands: /codex:review, /codex:adversarial-review, /codex:rescue, /codex:status, /codex:result, /codex:cancel, plus /codex:setup.

The plugin is NOT a deep MCP-style integration. It uses subprocess execution via a Node.js companion script (codex-companion.mjs) that communicates with the Codex app-server via JSON-RPC 2.0 over a Unix socket (macOS/Linux) or named pipe (Windows).
Installation requires Node.js 18.18+ and either a ChatGPT subscription (including free tier) or an OpenAI API key. Four steps: /plugin marketplace add, /plugin install, /reload-plugins, /codex:setup.

The plugin has TWO active Windows-blocking bugs as of April 2026: Issue #116 (spawn ENOENT prevents adversarial-review) and Issue #113 (install errors with corrupted messages). No built-in workaround exists.

The review gate (Stop hook) has a silent configuration bug (Issue #59): /codex:setup --enable-review-gate writes state to a temp directory, but the Stop hook reads from a different persistent directory. The gate appears enabled but never activates.

/codex:rescue has a hard 5-minute (300 second) timeout. Results are lost even if Codex completed analysis. Triggers on diffs with 30+ files or ~2,000+ lines — exactly the cases where review adds most value.

Both /codex:review and /codex:adversarial-review are read-only. They analyze but never apply changes. /codex:review accepts no focus text or custom instructions (not steerable). Only /codex:adversarial-review accepts custom focus text.

The plugin's authentication validator only accepts OpenAI OAuth tokens. Azure OpenAI, LiteLLM, Bedrock, and custom openai_base_url configurations report 'not authenticated' even when Codex CLI works fine with them (Issue #58).
For cloud tasks, OpenAI's Codex clones the connected GitHub repository and checks it out into an OpenAI-managed container. The full repository is uploaded to OpenAI's cloud, not just a diff.

API key users are NOT used for training by default (opt-out default). ChatGPT Free/Plus/Pro subscribers may have data used for training unless they opt out. Codex has a SEPARATE 'full environments' training toggle independent of ChatGPT's general privacy settings.

A patched critical vulnerability (BeyondTrust, Feb 2026) allowed GitHub OAuth token theft via unsanitized branch names during Codex cloud execution. Patch confirmed applied across all platforms. No active exploitation reported.

Codex CLI sends anonymous usage telemetry by default (opt-out). Data includes feature flags, review command counts, approval acceptance rates. Official docs confirm: 'None of those will contain code coming from your codebase.'
Codex has no standalone subscription. It is bundled with ChatGPT plans: Free ($0, temporary promo), Plus ($20), Pro ($200), Business ($30/user). Usage limits operate on a 5-hour rolling window.

All Codex API calls from the plugin count against the user's OpenAI plan limits, independently of Anthropic billing. No cross-service credit system exists. Both Claude Code and Codex accrue costs from their respective accounts.
Multi-model adversarial review detected 80% of known bugs vs. 53% for the best single model alone. Hardest system-level bugs: 100% detection in debate mode (5 rounds, structured benchmark).

The review gate creates a feedback loop where Codex's output influences Claude's next actions. Both companies receive the same codebase content through different pathways when the review gate is enabled.

The review gate is NOT recommended for SoNash. It conflicts with the existing loop-detector.js hook, adds uncontrolled Codex API cost per hook trigger, and would generate SoNash-context-unaware false positives.

SoNash's existing /pr-review skill is already tool-agnostic (AI_REVIEW_PROCESS.md v3.0 states 'Future tools — Process applies to any AI-based code review system'). Codex review output can be fed into it as a 'Mixed' source without skill changes.

A Level 1 SKILL.md approach (single markdown file in .claude/skills/) creates a functional Claude-Codex review loop without the plugin. One practitioner reported 14 issues detected in 3 rounds with zero additional tooling.
OpenAI Codex is powered by gpt-5.4 (flagship, SWE-Bench Pro 57.7%), gpt-5.4-mini (fast/cheap), gpt-5.3-codex (coding specialist), and gpt-5.3-codex-spark (Pro-only research preview).
OpenAI Codex is an asynchronous cloud-based software engineering agent integrated into ChatGPT, launched as research preview May 16, 2025. It is NOT the old 2021 Codex language model.
By March 2026, Codex had over 2 million weekly active users — a fivefold increase since early 2026.
OpenAI unified all Codex surfaces (CLI, VS Code, desktop, web) under a single App Server binary using JSON-RPC streamed as JSONL over stdio. Three primitives: Item, Turn, Thread.

OpenAI rejected MCP for Codex internal surface unification because 'maintaining MCP semantics in a way that made sense for VS Code proved difficult.' The richer session semantics (streaming diffs, approval flows, thread persistence) did not map onto MCP's tool-oriented model.
Codex reads AGENTS.md, an open standard also used by Cursor and Aider. Claude Code reads CLAUDE.md, which only works within Anthropic tools. Teams using both must maintain two separate config files.
The Claude Code plugin system launched in public beta October 9, 2025. It is now stable. Plugins can bundle commands, skills, agents, hooks, MCP servers, LSP servers, and executables (bin/).
Plugin agents cannot use hooks, mcpServers, or permissionMode in their frontmatter. The only valid isolation value is 'worktree'. This is a deliberate sandboxing restriction.
The plugin reached 11,000+ GitHub stars and 567 forks within 3 days of launch (by April 3, 2026). Described as 'catching on like wildfire' across multiple sources.
Anthropic has made no public statement about the plugin — no endorsement, collaboration claim, or objection. This absence is noted explicitly across multiple independent articles.

Issue #1 on the codex-plugin-cc repo (submitted Day 1, March 30, 2026) is a PR adding Gemini CLI extension support. OpenAI staff engaged with it constructively. Multi-model interoperability is a Day-1 community priority.
70% of developers now use 2-4 AI tools simultaneously; 15% use 5 or more. Gartner reported a 1,445% surge in multi-agent system inquiries from Q1 2024 to Q2 2025.
GitHub Agent HQ (February 2026) enables developers to run Claude, Codex, and Copilot simultaneously on the same task, institutionalizing multi-model as a platform feature with unified governance.
Claude Code has an estimated $2.5 billion annualized run rate and generates ~135,000 daily GitHub commits (~4% of all public commits). This market position prompted OpenAI's strategic response.

OpenAI's internal response to Claude Code's success was reportedly described as a 'wake-up call' by Fidji Simo (CEO of Applications). The plugin is a distribution play: API revenue without requiring tool switching.

SoNash skills (.claude/skills/), agents (.claude/agents/), and hooks are directly convertible to shareable Claude Code plugins. The official docs include explicit migration guidance. The primary behavioral change is namespacing (e.g., /deep-research becomes /sonash:deep-research).
API token pricing: GPT-5.4 costs $2.50 input / $10.00 output per 1M tokens; GPT-5.4-mini costs $0.40 input / $1.60 output. Claude Sonnet 4.6 costs $3 input / $15 output per 1M tokens.

Codex reportedly uses 3.2x-4.2x fewer tokens than Claude Code for equivalent tasks (community benchmarks, not official figures). At subscription rates, this efficiency difference is absorbed by flat-rate plans.

The Free tier Codex promotion is time-limited and should not be relied upon for sustained workflow integration. The plugin README's claim that Free tier works is accurate as of April 2026 but not a permanent state.

Issue #72 (community PR, open as of April 2026) implements bidirectional model-switching allowing individual Claude Code agents to run on OpenAI models via a 'thin-forwarder' pattern. If merged, significantly expands the plugin's capability profile.

The app-server-broker.mjs maintains a persistent session with the Codex app-server to avoid startup overhead per request, and multiplexes multiple concurrent review jobs. The Codex binary is spawned with detached:true and unref() so it survives parent process termination.

Configuration cascades from user-level (~/.codex/config.toml) to project-level (.codex/config.toml). Key options: model, model_reasoning_effort (none/minimal/low/medium/high/xhigh), openai_base_url. Per-command overrides available.

The plugin has 85+ open issues as of April 2026. Active runtime crashes include Issue #120 (EAGAIN hook crash), Issue #119 ('unknown variant' error), Issue #115 (infinite loop), Issue #117 (unhandled exception).

One real-world practitioner report found 4 correctness issues (silent no-ops, state race conditions, premature navigation, orphaned requests) in a Chrome extension redesign that Claude had not surfaced — semantic/behavioral bugs, not syntactic anti-patterns.

Credential storage: Codex supports 'keyring' (OS credential store, highest security), 'file' (~/.codex/auth.json, lowest), and 'auto' (default; keychain if available else file). auth.json should be treated like a password.

DNS-based covert data exfiltration channel was discovered in ChatGPT's code execution runtime by Check Point (patched March 2026). Combined with the BeyondTrust GitHub token vulnerability, this establishes a pattern of serious data leakage vectors in OpenAI cloud execution environments.
SoNash's /pr-review skill's DAS scoring framework and '2+ sources = elevate severity' rule naturally accommodate a Codex source. No skill changes required to process Codex review output.

Codex's 1M token context window (GPT-5.4) vs Claude's 200K default means routing large-codebase reviews through the plugin to Codex may yield qualitatively more complete coverage for large repositories.

Codex plugin: openai_base_url config option can redirect to Azure OpenAI, LiteLLM proxies, or other OpenAI-compatible endpoints. However, the auth validator bug (Issue #58) may cause 'not authenticated' errors in these configurations.
